reasoning: Pre-election season in Colombia (approaching May 2026) strongly correlates with an uptick in strategic positioning by armed groups—including non-aggression pact breakdowns, political violence threats (like by Ivan Mordisco for the 2026 elections), and state troop deployments. The ongoing collapse of the "Total Peace" initiative also drives up "Strategic developments" events such as arrest warrants, military operations, and ceasefire renegotiations. Since the baseline (73.583 events/30 days) is averaged over a year that was largely not an election year, the pre-election months (Jan/Feb/Mar 2026) are highly likely to exceed this baseline average. I expect the 30-day counts leading up to both resolution dates to exceed ~73.6. Uncertainty increases slightly for the latter date.
